todaysEvents.summary: Iranian strike injures 15 US soldiers in Kuwait (Apr 6, J.Post) - first confirmed US military casualties in Kuwait; marks direct Iran-US kinetic contact on Gulf soil; escalation beyond aircraft losses.

Iran pushes peace proposal to end war; Trump rejects it: 'not good enough, Tuesday deadline final' (J.Post, Al Jazeera Apr 6); ISW Apr 5 confirms deadline extended to Apr 7 8pm ET; Brent holding near $111 est. ahead of binary trigger.
todaysEvents.delta: Iran proposal rejected; Apr 7 deadline final

todaysEvents.summary: IDF strikes Asaluyeh petrochemical complex (South Pars-linked) + kills IRGC intel chief Majid Khademi (J.Post Apr 6); IRGC blocks 2 Qatari LNG tankers at Hormuz despite Iran-US transit agreement (Reuters Apr 6); AIS transits -95%; Apr 7 8pm ET deadline active.
todaysEvents.delta: IDF hits Asaluyeh; Qatari LNG tankers blocked

scenarios.label: Base
scenarios.description: April 7 8pm ET deadline passes with Trump accepting Iran's proposal framework (rejected Apr 6 but renegotiated): partial Hormuz reopening, toll booth suspended as gesture, nuclear inspection access granted. Kuwait water crisis creates mutual civilian-harm pressure. Pakistan/Qatar back-channel produces bridging agreement. Brent retreats to $98-105/bbl; JKM pulls back to $17-19/MMBtu on Qatar damage assessment resuming. P&I cover only partially restored - toll booth precedent means full commercial Hormuz passage requires a separate legal framework.

scenarios.label: Stress
scenarios.description: April 7 8pm ET deadline passes with limited strikes on Iranian power grid and bridges; Iran absorbs hits but maintains toll booth and bilateral deal framework. Kuwait desalination offline for weeks; GCC civilian infrastructure attacks continue; 15 US soldiers injured in Kuwait Apr 6 raises Congressional pressure. US-Israel avoid Kharg seizure; Pakistan-brokered ceasefire talks drag into May. IRGC cyberattacks on Western tech firms cause selective disruptions. Brent settles $105-130/bbl; JKM at $19-24/MMBtu; iTraxx Asia IG holds 195-215bp.

scenarios.label: Tail
scenarios.description: April 7 8pm ET strikes on Kharg Island or power plants trigger full Hormuz corridor closure and ground-war-scale Iranian response. 15 US soldiers already injured in Kuwait Apr 6 - escalation accelerating. Kuwait water crisis deepens; Saudi Aramco infrastructure targeted; UAE attacks overwhelm interception systems. IRGC executes cyberattacks on US tech firms (Apple, Nvidia supply chains). Houthis escalate to daily strikes; ground invasion preparations confirmed. A-10 and F-15E losses signal Iran's improved air-defence capability. Ras Laffan offline for the quarter; Saudi output cuts 3-5M bpd. Oil hits $160-185/bbl; LNG spot markets seize. ASEAN recession risk critical.
